body.title: Daemon supervisor: last-spawn-timestamp liveness check for one-shot-per-tick workers
body.result: heartbeat-based liveness check: supervisor.heartbeat(name) at spawn-hook entry, staleness_threshold_secs per entry, stale flagged in report_str. one-shot entries no longer in dead-thread warning. report-only, no restart.flag. ne-worker=600s, consolidation=7200s. 9 new tests in test_daemon_supervisor.py, 3947 pass.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
ne-worker and consolidation-worker are one-shot-per-tick: the Igor main loop spawns a fresh thread each tick, runs the work once, thread exits. Registering them with one_shot=True (current state, post-revert) silences the supervisor's DAEMON_DEAD alert — which was the right call because natural exit is the design — but leaves a real hole: if the main loop stops calling the spawn entrypoint (stuck, deadlocked, exception in the spawn guard), no thread is dying, so is_alive() never fires, so the supervisor never notices. The original T-daemon-supervisor-backoff-and-one-shot-audit tried to close this hole by removing one_shot=True; that broke boot instead (see the 2026-04-24 crash loop). Correct fix: keep one_shot=True, add a separate liveness_fn per entry. For one-shot-per-tick workers, liveness_fn checks (now - last_spawn_ts) < threshold. Supervisor reports unhealthy (not DEAD) if liveness fails.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/cognition/daemon_supervisor.py (add last_spawn_ts field + liveness_fn registration API), wild_igor/igor/main.py (thread the last-spawn-ts update into _maybe_run_ne and _run_consolidation_background), tests/test_daemon_supervisor.py (new or extend). **Design rules:** docs-live-in-code (module docstring update), test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — supervisor API extension, two call-site updates, liveness_fn that compares wall-clock to stored last-spawn-ts, report_str() shows stale status. OUT — changing ne-worker/consolidation to a true long-running while-loop (separate design), auto-restart on staleness (just report, don't act). **Test plan:** unit test supervisor: register with last_spawn_ts getter, advance mock clock past threshold, assert report shows stale; advance clock under threshold, assert healthy.
